lesson.menu_desc: Field questions and close a presentation with confidence: invite, defer, and answer questions, then deliver a strong closing -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Handling Questions & Closing the Talk
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Diogo Leal | Aula 15 -- Handling Questions

listenings.text: Hi Diogo, it is Sophia. Last week you described the data on your chart slide very clearly. Now let us work on the end of the talk: the questions and the closing. After your last slide, invite questions. Say, are there any questions before I close? When someone asks, listen to the whole query first. If it is a good point, say that is a fair point, thank you. If you do not have the answer right now, do not panic. You can defer it. Say, let me come back to that after the meeting. If a question repeats an assumption that is wrong, give a short, calm response and move on. Do not let one query take over the room. At the end, give a brief summary, thank the audience, and close with a clear next step. A strong closing is short. You recall the main message, you thank everyone, and you stop. Practice the closing line until it feels natural. You are ready. Talk soon.

Before I finish, let me open the floor. Are there any questions? Yes, that is a fair point about the timeline. The new tool will save us two weeks, so the date is realistic. That is a good query, but let me come back to that after the meeting, because it needs more detail. To recall the main message: revenue is up, costs are down, and Project Atlas is on track. I appreciate your time and your sharp questions today. The next step is simple. I will send a brief summary by email this afternoon, and we will meet again next month. Thank you all very much. Have a great day.

extra_audio.text: Diogo reaches his last slide and invites questions from the room. First, he listens to a query and answers it with a short, calm response. Then, he defers a harder question and says he will come back to it after the meeting. After that, he recalls the main message of the talk in one sentence. Finally, he thanks the audience and gives a clear next step: a brief summary by email.
